stage1.theme_summary: Over the last 12 months Bigdata.com evidence confirms a structural break is now underway in consulting economics — not a cyclical dip. Three independent signal tracks converge: (1) News & analyst chatter has escalated from speculation to confirmed revenue impairment. Accenture posted its worst single-day drop on record on June 18 2026, falling 20% after cutting full-year guidance and retiring its 'advanced AI bookings' disclosure — a metric analysts read as management conceding AI is no longer a revenue tailwind. Morgan Stanley cut its ACN price target from $240 to $130 in six months and downgraded twice. The Lighthouse Canton research note, published June 23, declared the consultant moat structurally dissolved: 'Generative and agentic AI is the first wave engineered to collapse that gap... this is a contraction of demand, not a deferral.' (2) SEC & international filing disclosures have escalated sharply. Wipro's FY2026 20-F (filed Jun 2026) is now the most explicit filing in the sector — naming AI-driven automation, client self-service, and pricing compression as direct adverse revenue scenarios in plain language, not legalese. Infosys FY2026 20-F (filed Jun 15) matches this. Cognizant FY2025 10-K and Accenture FY2025 10-K both upgraded AI demand reduction from opportunity framing to named material risk. (3) Structural distress at the bottom of the pyramid is spreading. DXC Technology — a mid-tier IT services firm — saw organic revenue fall 5% in FY2026, guided to another 3-5% decline in FY2027, shed ~40% of its share price over the past year, and now faces securities fraud investigation from Pomerantz Law. McKinsey (private) announced plans to cut a further 10% of non-client-facing staff over 18-24 months on top of prior reductions. The correlation between large-firm AI risk disclosure language and small/mid-tier firm actual revenue distress is the key insight: the risk language is predictive, not retrospective.

impact_direction_map.Revenue Demand Destruction: Lighthouse Canton (Jun 23) and Morgan Stanley (Jun 22) both characterize AI as creating a structural contraction of consulting demand — not a deferral. When clients can self-serve with AI, the engagement scope permanently shrinks. Accenture's managed services bookings fell 15% YoY in Q3 FY2026.
impact_direction_map.Pyramid Economics Collapse: The billable-hours pyramid — wide junior base cross-subsidizing senior partners — is being hollowed out from below. McKinsey CEO Sternfels revealed a '20,000-agent workforce' alongside 40,000 humans, targeting 1 agent per human. KPMG is already shifting juniors to supervising AI agents on its Velocity platform.
impact_direction_map.Pricing Model Forced Migration: McKinsey ~25% of fees are now outcome-based. Cognizant is testing 'tokenised pricing' (human/AI agent blend). Outcome-based transition is structurally margin-dilutive in the near term: clients' first question on fixed-price deals is 'where is my AI cost saving?'
impact_direction_map.Disclosure Escalation as Leading Indicat: Wipro FY2026 20-F is the most unambiguous: 'AI-driven automation… may reduce demand… adversely affect pricing, margins… clients may develop internal AI capabilities.' This language appeared in FY2025 filings as boilerplate; its specificity now signals management conviction on the risk.
impact_direction_map.Mid/Small-Tier First Casualties: DXC Technology: organic revenue −5% FY2026, guided −3–5% FY2027, stock −40% in 12 months, securities fraud probe launched. Wipro and Tech Mahindra both reported actual revenue declines in FY2025. These are the canaries — large firms will follow as long-term managed services contracts roll off.
impact_direction_map.Deal Push-Out Mechanism: Accenture management flagged two large managed services deals worth $300-500M each pushed to FY2027 as 'client-specific.' Morgan Stanley reads this as structural: clients in a deflationary AI environment are shortening contract tenors, deferring commitments, and buying in 3-month increments rather than 3-year ones.
impact_direction_map.Relative Resilience: Strategy vs IT Deli: BCG remains the clear relative winner — AI accounts for ~20% of revenue and growing. FTI Consulting's Q1 2026 earnings call noted AI is generating 'entirely new categories of work' around disputes, bankruptcy, and regulatory enforcement. Short-duration expert-judgment work is more insulated than long-duration labor-delivery contracts.
impact_direction_map.Book-to-Bill as the Canary Metric: Accenture's book-to-bill is at 1.0 — at threshold. Lighthouse Canton's bear case: 'We expect book-to-bill to print below 1.0 at the bellwether within one to two quarters.' A sub-1.0 book-to-bill compounds: each quarter below replacement lowers the base, so deceleration accelerates rather than mean-reverts.

timeline.event: Baseline: Indian IT FY2025 results show Wipro −2.72%, Tech Mahindra −0.21% — first cracks in pyramid economics
Accenture FY2025 10-K filed: AI named explicit material risk; headcount cut 11,000+ to 779k. KPMG appoints Bret Greenstein as global AI strategy lead
Industry survey: 65% of enterprises say traditional consulting 'no longer delivers value'. Outcome-based pricing projected to dominate within 2 years (Benzinga/Nov)
McKinsey plans 10% non-client staff cuts (Bloomberg). FT: salary freezes across top consultancies. Cognizant, Capgemini headcount near-flat for 3rd consecutive quarter
McKinsey CEO reveals 20,000 AI agents alongside 40,000 humans; targets 1:1 ratio. Says 'we're migrating away from pure advisory to outcomes-based'
Cognizant 10-K FY2025 filed: AI demand reduction explicitly named as material risk. Capgemini Q4 2025 annual report names Agentic AI as emerging displacement risk
Avendsus Spark research: sector headcount additions near zero across TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture for 3 consecutive quarters
FTI Consulting Q1 2026 earnings: AI generating entirely new dispute/litigation categories. McKinsey ~25% of fees now outcome-based
FT: 'How AI is forcing McKinsey and peers to rethink pricing.' DXC Q4 FY2026: organic revenue −6.6%; bookings −13.5%; securities fraud probe launched. Big Four KPMG cuts hundreds of advisory staff

INFLECTION: Accenture Jun 18 — worst single-day drop on record (−20%). Morgan Stanley PT $240→$130. Capgemini −11%, Infosys −6%, Wipro −7.3%, Cognizant −7%, Globant −13%, EPAM −11%, CGI −9%. Wipro 20-F filed Jun 2 with sector's most explicit AI risk language. Infosys 20-F Jun 15 matches it. Lighthouse Canton (Jun 23): 'This is a contraction of demand, not a deferral'

scenarios.name: Structural Demand Contraction (Bear — Lighthouse Canton Thesis)
scenarios.description: Agentic AI matures faster than consensus expects. Enterprise clients fully internalize AI tools within 18 months, permanently shrinking consulting scope to senior judgment only. Book-to-bill at Accenture prints below 1.0 within 2 quarters, triggering further estimate cuts. Mid-tier firms (DXC, Wipro, Tech Mahindra) face sustained multi-year revenue decline. Going-concern signals spread from small international IT boutiques to mid-caps. Outcome-based pricing transition destroys margin structure faster than volume offsets.

scenarios.name: Managed Transition — AI Augments, Not Replaces (Base)
scenarios.description: Large firms successfully pivot to AI-enabled delivery. Volume lost on junior delivery is partly offset by new demand for AI transformation advisory, governance, change management. Pricing erodes 15-20% but volume grows. McKinsey and BCG maintain margins on higher-value work. Mid-tier IT services firms restructure successfully (DXC OASIS platform). Book-to-bill stabilizes above 1.0. Stock multiples re-rate modestly higher from depressed levels by mid-2027.

scenarios.name: AI Demand Offset — New Categories Exceed Displacement (Bull)
scenarios.description: OpenAI Frontier Alliance (McKinsey, BCG, Accenture, Capgemini) generates massive new advisory revenue as enterprises need help scaling AI from pilots to production. 95% of GenAI pilots failing to deliver ROI (MIT stat cited by IBM) drives demand for implementation expertise. Accenture AI bookings accelerate, book-to-bill returns to 1.2x+. Indian IT firms win large AI infrastructure contracts. Consulting sector re-rates upward.

evidence.headline: Accenture warns of lower revenue growth as AI threatens IT consultancy
evidence.chunk_text: Accenture shares were on course to open at their lowest level since 2018 after the consulting group cut its revenue forecast, stoking investor fears that the rapid advance of AI is undermining traditional IT and outsourcing businesses. New bookings fell to $19.3bn, a decline of 3% in local currency. It forecast revenue growth of a maximum of 4%, below its previous prediction of 3-5%. Chief executive Julie Sweet has argued that Accenture is winning business from companies seeking help to adopt AI, but investors fret that the technology will allow clients to cut consultants out or create new competition from AI start-ups.
evidence.search_topic: Accenture revenue guidance cut AI June 2026

evidence.headline: Consulting's Only Deliverable: A Value Trap — Lighthouse Canton
evidence.chunk_text: Generative and agentic AI is the first wave engineered to collapse that gap: the interface is natural language, the documentation writes itself, and the implementer and end-user become the same person. When a finance team can stand up an agentic workflow by describing it, the integrator is disintermediated from the build-and-run layers — exactly where the headcount and the revenue live. And unlike cloud or ERP there is no multi-year migration to staff: adoption is incremental, self-served and reversible, so even the transition revenue the industry has always harvested between platforms largely fails to materialise. This is a contraction of demand, not a deferral.
evidence.search_topic: Agentic AI consulting disintermediation 2026

evidence.headline: Accenture's Bookings Decline Clouds Visibility Into Fiscal 2027 — Morgan Stanley
evidence.chunk_text: Morgan Stanley said the Managed Services bookings decline of 15% may be partly explained by AI continuing to crowd out IT spend. Investors will need to see improved Managed Services bookings to gain confidence that the push-outs were idiosyncratic rather than a sign of softening demand. Morgan Stanley lowered its fiscal 2026 revenue forecast and its price target to $130 from $177, having already downgraded to equal-weight from overweight. FY2027 EPS estimated at $14.44.
evidence.search_topic: Morgan Stanley Accenture downgrade AI bookings

evidence.headline: William Blair downgrades Accenture, sees 'penalty box' persisting
evidence.chunk_text: William Blair downgraded Accenture to Market Perform from Outperform. Q3 results, guidance, and commentary point to weakening forward demand and a softer implied exit rate into fiscal 2027. Blair says with slowing bookings, accelerating acquisition dilution, demand headwinds in the Middle East, and 'seemingly no boost' to the consulting business from AI so far, Accenture shares are 'likely to remain in the penalty box.' The stock fell 19% to $126.50.
evidence.search_topic: Accenture revenue guidance cut AI June 2026

evidence.headline: Accenture Slides After Revenue Miss and Reduced Full-Year Forecast
evidence.chunk_text: Revenue increased 6% year-over-year to $18.7 billion but came in below expectations. The company recently announced it would no longer separately disclose artificial intelligence bookings — a metric closely monitored by investors seeking evidence of AI-related demand. Bookings of $19.3 billion came in broadly flat, compared with analyst forecasts calling for growth of approximately 7%.
evidence.search_topic: Accenture advanced AI bookings disclosure retired

evidence.headline: Accenture Faces Pressure as AI Spending Crowds Out IT Services Budgets — Morgan Stanley
evidence.chunk_text: Morgan Stanley Q1 CIO Survey points to just ~2% YoY growth in IT services spending in 2026 while overall IT budget growth remains unchanged amid increasing AI prioritisation. Fiscal Q3 bookings could fall short of expectations, with a 'diminishing' benefit from managed services contracts. Investor concerns: weaker bookings, risk of future estimate cuts, fears that AI-driven productivity gains could eventually weigh on demand for services firms. Morgan Stanley downgraded ACN to equal-weight from overweight, cutting target to $177 from $240.
evidence.search_topic: Morgan Stanley Accenture downgrade AI bookings

evidence.headline: TCS overtakes Accenture for first time since 2021 after historic selloff
evidence.chunk_text: The market reaction reverberated across the global technology services sector after Accenture warned that revenue growth could slow as artificial intelligence reshapes parts of its business. IBM and Cognizant fell nearly 7% each. US-listed Infosys declined 6%, Wipro ADRs fell 7.3%. Capgemini dropped 11% and Sopra Steria lost 6.8%. Globant fell 13%, EPAM Systems dropped 11%, and CGI lost more than 9% in Toronto trading.
evidence.search_topic: IT consulting sector selloff Capgemini Infosys TCS Wipro

evidence.headline: WIPRO LTD files FORM 20-F for FY 2026
evidence.chunk_text: AI-driven automation, efficiency gains and increased client self-service may reduce demand for certain services we provide and may adversely affect pricing, margins, service levels or the mix of services delivered. The adoption of AI may also intensify competition and lower barriers to entry, enabling competitors, including traditional IT service providers, hyperscale software vendors, startups and other market participants, to offer similar or substitute services more efficiently or at lower cost. In addition, some clients may develop or expand internal AI capabilities rather than rely on third-party service providers, which could further reduce demand for our services and adversely affect our revenues and market position.
evidence.search_topic: Wipro 20-F FY2026 AI self-service pricing margins

evidence.headline: Infosys Ltd files FORM 20-F for FY 2026
evidence.chunk_text: Increased adoption of AI and automation technologies has and may continue to reduce demand for certain traditional technology services, alter client purchasing behavior, compress pricing and margins, reduce demand for labor-based delivery models and increase expectations for productivity improvements and outcome-based pricing. In addition, clients may increasingly develop in-house AI capabilities or rely on lower-cost competitors, open-source technologies or AI platform providers, which could further reduce demand for our services.
evidence.search_topic: Infosys 20-F FY2026 AI demand reduction labor model

evidence.headline: Accenture plc files FORM 10-K for FY 2025
evidence.chunk_text: AI technologies are complex and rapidly evolving, and we face significant competition from clients and ecosystem partners who may develop their own internal AI-related capabilities, as well as new AI-native companies, which can lead to reduced demand for our solutions or services. As these technologies evolve, some services and tasks currently performed by our people have been and will continue to be replaced by automation, including AI-enabled solutions, which will lead to reduced demand for our services and/or adversely affect the utilization rate of our professionals.
evidence.search_topic: Cognizant 10-K FY2025 AI material risk demand reduction

evidence.headline: COGNIZANT TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS CORP files FORM 10-K for FY 2025
evidence.chunk_text: We expect the proliferation of AI will have a significant impact on our industry, and we believe our ability to compete in this space will be critical to our financial performance. As AI-based technologies or other forms of automation evolve, demand for some services that we currently perform for our clients may be reduced and our ability to obtain favorable pricing or other terms for some of our services may be diminished.
evidence.search_topic: Cognizant 10-K FY2025 AI material risk demand reduction

evidence.headline: McKinsey challenges graduates to use AI chatbot in recruitment overhaul
evidence.chunk_text: McKinsey is also planning more job cuts, in part to reflect efficiencies from AI — reportedly targeting 10% of non-client facing roles over the next two years. McKinsey CEO Sternfels told a podcast the firm had a 'workforce' of 20,000 agents on top of its 40,000 staff. That figure would grow over the next 18 months to 'get to one agent per human.' 'We're migrating pretty quickly away from pure advisory work to much more of an outcomes-based model. The stuff that I did when I joined as an associate 32 years ago, we wouldn't consider even doing right now. Because clients do that stuff themselves.'
evidence.search_topic: McKinsey headcount reduction AI automation 2025 2026

evidence.headline: How AI is forcing McKinsey and its peers to rethink pricing
evidence.chunk_text: McKinsey is under pressure from clients to tie its fees to outcomes achieved rather than to hours its consultants spend. Charging for results rather than work done makes revenue less reliable, which helps explain why the firm will shunt a bigger share of partners' pay into equity. The drive to tie fees to outcomes partly reflects the way billable hours are becoming less useful as a yardstick, thanks to consultants' own usage of AI for tasks such as data analysis and diagnosis.
evidence.search_topic: Billable hour outcome-based pricing AI consulting

evidence.headline: How AI threatens the giants of consulting
evidence.chunk_text: Roughly a third of McKinsey's work is now tied to performance-based fees. 'AI is really challenging the billable hour model that a lot of the firms have been built on.' For firms built around audit practices, the transition is particularly difficult. Independence rules and longstanding cultural norms make success-based billing more complicated for the Big Four. The shift away from hourly billing is also undermining the traditional pyramid structure, in which a firm employs thousands of junior-level employees and thins out the ranks with an up-or-out promotion culture.
evidence.search_topic: Consulting AI pyramid model junior staff collapse

evidence.headline: Will the IT consulting share price rout ever end?
evidence.chunk_text: Accenture shares have more than halved in less than 18 months. 'What the current stock price is reflecting is a pretty significant degree of concern that they are a net loser from AI,' said Jason Kupferberg, IT services analyst at Wells Fargo Securities. Cognizant is down 43% from its high. Infosys and Tata Consultancy Services are down 41% and 49% respectively. Cognizant is experimenting with 'tokenised' pricing that tiers fees based on the balance of human and AI agents working on a project.
evidence.search_topic: IT consulting sector selloff Capgemini Infosys TCS Wipro

evidence.headline: McKinsey layoffs show white-collar job cuts are spreading
evidence.chunk_text: McKinsey & Co. is preparing to cut its own workforce — as much as 10% of headcount within some areas, a move that could eliminate several thousand jobs over the next 18 to 24 months. After a multi-year hiring spree pushing McKinsey's workforce above 45,000, revenue growth has stalled. The firm now employs closer to 40,000 people, and utilization rates across the industry have reportedly sagged as corporate clients trim budgets, delay projects, and opt for automation and AI over consulting services.
evidence.search_topic: McKinsey headcount reduction AI automation 2025 2026

evidence.headline: Pomerantz Law investigates DXC Technology — securities fraud probe
evidence.chunk_text: DXC reported total revenue of ~$3.13 billion for Q4, a 1.2% YoY decline and a 6.6% decline on an organic basis. Bookings ~$3.3 billion, down 13.5% YoY. Management disclosed that DXC's top-line performance fell short of expectations — 'not just a pipeline and demand issue, but also an execution issue.' DXC issued FY2027 guidance projecting continued organic revenue decline of ~3% to 5% YoY.
evidence.search_topic: DXC Technology organic revenue decline AI disruption

evidence.headline: DXC Technology Co.: Investor Day
evidence.chunk_text: For a period of time, too much of our offering portfolio, our skill base and our sales focus were aligned to parts of the market that were structurally declining. We saw traditional workplace delivery shift toward automation, custom applications moving to enterprise applications. That market transition created persistent headwinds to our top line. Revenues have declined, and prior customer terminations related to these shifts continue to affect reported performance well beyond the point when those initial decisions were made.
evidence.search_topic: DXC Technology organic revenue decline AI disruption

evidence.headline: Global Management Consulting Industry June 2026 — GSBR Research
evidence.chunk_text: Competitive intensity is likely to rise as AI lowers the cost of research, synthesis and prototype development. New AI-native advisory models and software-led transformation providers can deliver narrower workstreams faster and at lower cost, putting pressure on traditional pyramid staffing and hourly billing. The implication is not that consulting demand disappears, but that differentiation increasingly shifts toward senior judgment, sector expertise, change management, trusted relationships and the ability to operationalise AI safely inside complex organizations. McKinsey indicates roughly a quarter of its global fees now come from results-based arrangements.
evidence.search_topic: Consulting AI pyramid model junior staff collapse

evidence.headline: FTI CONSULTING files FORM 8-K — AI generates new work categories
evidence.chunk_text: We are already finding that AI is generating entirely new categories of work. We are supporting clients in a new set of high-profile disputes which involve AI companies — ownership of AI-generated content, harm caused by AI misinformation and bias, unauthorized use of data, and privacy concerns. We believe the rapid pace of AI innovation will be one of the most disruptive events in our lifetime. And that disruption is and will drive demand for our experts. The more disruption happens from AI, the more those sorts of things are going to happen — whether it's crisis communications, bankruptcies, or investigations.
evidence.search_topic: Expert/Disputes consulting AI beneficiary

evidence.headline: AI-Powered Consulting Forces Reckoning: 65% of Enterprises Say Traditional Models No Longer Deliver Value
evidence.chunk_text: Only 13% of leaders rated traditional consulting as 'highly effective,' revealing a major trust gap. AI-powered consulting usage is projected to triple within two years, from 12% to 35% of services delivered. Outcome-based pricing is set to become the dominant model, leapfrogging headcount-based contracts within two years.
evidence.search_topic: Billable hour outcome-based pricing AI consulting

evidence.headline: Top consultancies freeze starting salaries as AI threatens 'pyramid' model
evidence.chunk_text: PwC cut 150 back-office staff in the US. McKinsey cut 200 IT jobs globally. Accenture reduced its global headcount by more than 11,000 to 779,000 in the three months to August, and has said it will cut jobs of staff it believes cannot be retrained to use AI. Some former Big Four partners are founding AI-native boutiques that say they will use the technology to replace much of the traditional junior cohort. 'Clients are quite legitimately asking, what are you doing? That's become a new credentialisation, to explain how you are applying AI to your firm.'
evidence.search_topic: Consulting AI pyramid model junior staff collapse

evidence.headline: Cognizant scripts turnaround with fastest growth in four years
evidence.chunk_text: Cognizant grew 6.95% in calendar year 2025 to $21.12bn — best since 2021. But Tata Consultancy Services and Wipro ended the year with revenues down 0.72% and 1.41% respectively. Wipro ended at $10.42bn. Indian IT sector results are diverging sharply, with Cognizant and HCL outperforming while TCS and Wipro contracted.
evidence.search_topic: Wipro Infosys TCS Cognizant revenue actuals FY2025

evidence.headline: Avendus Spark IT Industry Update — AI changes the stack, not the relevance of vendors
evidence.chunk_text: Headcount additions remained near zero or negative across Accenture, TCS, Infosys, Wipro for three consecutive quarters. Revenue/employee could see an increase as outcome-based pricing mix rises, but pricing pressure over medium to long term as AI adoption improves at scale. IT vendors would navigate this with pyramid structure changes and improved internal productivity.
evidence.search_topic: Consulting sector book-to-bill deterioration

taxonomy.name: Direct Revenue Destruction Mechanisms
taxonomy.description: The specific pathways through which AI reduces consulting firm revenues in the 12-month window.

subnodes.name: Client Self-Sufficiency
subnodes.description: Clients internalising AI tools to self-perform research, diagnostics, and slide production — permanently shrinking addressable consulting scope. Named explicitly in Wipro 20-F FY2026 and Infosys 20-F FY2026.

subnodes.name: Junior Delivery Automation
subnodes.description: LLMs and agentic AI executing the standardised analytical work that sustains large junior cohorts. McKinsey's Lilli tool writes decks; KPMG's Velocity moves juniors to AI supervision roles.

subnodes.name: Deal Push-Outs and Tenor Compression
subnodes.description: Clients in a deflationary AI environment shorten commitment horizons. Accenture flagged two $300-500M deals slipping to FY2027. Morgan Stanley: 'clients that learn to buy in 3-month increments rarely revert to 3-year ones.'

subnodes.name: AI-Native Boutique Undercutting
subnodes.description: Former Big Four partners founding lean AI-native boutiques with outcome-based models and no junior overhead, competing on sub-segments of the large firm's work at lower cost.

taxonomy.name: Business Model Structural Fragility
taxonomy.description: Embedded features of consulting firm economics that amplify AI-driven revenue pressure.

subnodes.name: Billable Hour Cross-Subsidy Collapse
subnodes.description: Junior billable hours cross-subsidize partner economics. AI attacks the lowest-cost hours first, destroying the margin engine. 65% of enterprise clients already say traditional models no longer deliver value (Nov 2025 survey).

subnodes.name: Outcome-Based Pricing Margin Trap
subnodes.description: Transition to outcome pricing is necessary but corrosive: once price decouples from headcount, the client's first question is 'where are my AI savings?' — handing the productivity dividend back to buyers.

subnodes.name: Big Four Independence Rule Constraint
subnodes.description: FTI CEO (Q1 2026 earnings): success-based billing is 'anathema' to large professional services firms. Audit-linked independence rules prevent performance fee structures that pure consulting firms can adopt, slowing Big Four pivot speed.

taxonomy.name: Regulatory & Disclosure Signal Escalation
taxonomy.description: How AI risk language in official filings has shifted over the 12-month window as a forward indicator.

subnodes.name: Wipro 20-F FY2026 — Most Explicit Filing
subnodes.description: Filed Jun 2 2026. Names AI automation, efficiency gains, and client self-service as causes of demand reduction, pricing compression, and margin pressure. The specificity here is a step-change from boilerplate.

subnodes.name: Infosys 20-F FY2026 — Labor Model at Risk
subnodes.description: Filed Jun 15 2026. Explicitly names 'reduce demand for labor-based delivery models' — direct acknowledgement that the offshore pyramid is the asset at risk.

subnodes.name: ACN & CTSH 10-K Escalation
subnodes.description: Both FY2025 annual reports (Oct 2025, Feb 2026) upgraded AI demand reduction from opportunity language to named material risk. Accenture also retired its 'advanced AI bookings' metric in Jun 2026 — treated by analysts as a disclosure of deteriorating data.

subnodes.name: DXC Securities Fraud Probe as Bottom-of-Pyramid Signal
subnodes.description: Pomerantz investigation (Jun 24 2026) into whether DXC concealed demand and bookings deterioration. A mid-tier firm facing securities fraud inquiry over AI-driven demand weakness is a structural bellwether for smaller firms.

taxonomy.name: Risk Stratification by Firm Type
taxonomy.description: Which firm categories are most and least exposed, based on 12-month evidence.

subnodes.name: Most Exposed: Large IT Delivery (ACN/CTSH/INFY/TCS/WIT)
subnodes.description: Headcount-heavy, hour-billed, large offshore pyramids. 5 of 5 now have explicit AI demand reduction language in annual filings. Stock prices down 40-50%+ from peaks.

subnodes.name: Most Distressed: Mid-Tier IT Services (DXC)
subnodes.description: Organic revenue contracting, bookings collapsing, securities probe launched. The structurally weakest large public IT services firm. Actual going-concern-adjacent financial stress, not just disclosure risk.

subnodes.name: Insulated: Expert/Disputes/Restructuring (FTI)
subnodes.description: AI-generated disputes, bankruptcies, and regulatory investigations are new demand categories. Short-duration expert judgment work with credentialed professionals — hardest to automate, most defensible.

subnodes.name: Relative Winner: Pure Strategy (BCG)
subnodes.description: AI ~20% of revenue, growing. Shorter engagements, higher judgment content, outcome-driven. Private so no stock signal but BCG is the clearest winner in the 12-month data.

taxonomy.name: Compounding Macro Headwinds
taxonomy.description: Non-AI factors amplifying AI disruption effects within the 12-month window.

subnodes.name: Middle East Conflict Impact
subnodes.description: Accenture guided ~$100M revenue impact from Middle East conflict in Q3 FY2026 — affecting discretionary consulting specifically. Creates noise in separating AI displacement from geopolitical deferral.

subnodes.name: US Federal Government Shutdown
subnodes.description: DHS shutdown (Feb 14 2026) impacted IBM Consulting and government-facing IT services firms. Adds ~100-200bp headwind to already-pressured sector.

subnodes.name: Enterprise AI Budget Reallocation
subnodes.description: Morgan Stanley CIO Survey: IT services spending ~2% growth in 2026 while overall IT budgets unchanged — meaning AI capex is cannibalising consulting spend within flat total budgets. Zero-sum displacement dynamic confirmed.
